_comment: Random world events that happen around each avatar type. Injected periodically to give the avatar an ongoing life. Each event is a shared experience + language teaching moment + future callback material.
street_food: A rat just ran across the alley and a tourist screamed — the vendor didn't even flinch. That's how you know it's a real local spot.
The dumpling lady next door is fighting with her supplier on the phone. Something about the price of pork going up. She's loud enough that everyone can hear.
Someone just burned their mouth on a soup dumpling and is doing that thing where they breathe in and out with their mouth open. Classic rookie mistake.
A food blogger just showed up with a ring light and the vendor is pretending not to notice but definitely stood up straighter.
It just started raining and everyone is crowding under the awnings. The smell of wet pavement mixing with grilled meat is actually incredible.
Two vendors across from each other are having a volume war — both yelling their prices louder and louder. The customers are loving it.
form_helper: Someone just got told they're in the wrong queue after waiting an hour. You can see their soul leaving their body.
The printer jammed again and three people are standing around it like it's a patient in surgery.
A woman just found out she needs a photo that's exactly 35x45mm and not 35x46mm. She's been here since 8am.
The security guard just winked at me — counter 4 opened early, nobody knows yet. Move fast.
Someone filled out the entire form in the wrong language and the clerk is trying SO hard to be patient.
pronunciation_tutor: The couple at the next table is trying to order and I can hear them pronouncing everything wrong. My hands are twitching.
A street musician outside is singing and their pronunciation is actually perfect — better than most of my students honestly.
Someone just walked in speaking the most beautiful dialect I've heard in months. I'm eavesdropping and I'm not sorry.
The barista just mispronounced my regular order and I'm trying to decide if I should correct them or let it go.
office_navigator: Someone just reply-all'd their lunch order to the entire company. 47 people now know they want extra pickles.
The boss just walked through and everyone suddenly looks extremely busy. The energy shift is unreal.
There's a birthday cake in the break room and people are hovering around it like sharks. Nobody wants to be the first to cut it.
The air conditioning broke on the hottest day of the year. Ties are coming off. Standards are falling.
market_haggler: A grandmother just haggled a vendor down 40% without breaking eye contact once. Absolute legend.
Someone's kid knocked over a pile of fruit and the vendor is pretending to be mad but clearly thinks it's hilarious.
A tourist just paid triple the real price and the vendor is trying not to smile. I physically cannot let this stand.
It's starting to rain and vendors are throwing tarps over everything while still shouting prices. Chaos but beautiful chaos.
The tea guy on the corner just gave me a free sample and now I owe him a conversation. This is his strategy and it works every time.
night_guide: The DJ just dropped something and half the room screamed. The other half is pretending they're too cool to react.
A group of tourists just walked in wearing matching shirts. The bartender and I made eye contact and we both know what's about to happen.
Someone just tried to order a cocktail in the local language and the bartender is genuinely impressed. That never happens.
The bouncer just let someone skip the line and three people in the queue are about to lose it.
It's 1am and the street food cart just set up outside. The timing is always perfect — they know exactly when everyone gets hungry.
elder_speaker: The grandchild just called and asked how to say something in the formal way for a job interview. This is the best phone call I'll have all week.
A neighbor's child just used modern slang that I'm 90% sure is an insult but delivered so sweetly that everyone nodded politely.
The tea is ready and the whole house smells like cardamom. Some things don't need to change.
Someone on TV just used a proverb wrong and I actually talked back to the television. My spouse pretended not to hear.
youth_translator: Someone just used slang from two years ago and I physically cringed. That's how you spot someone who learned from an app.
A new meme is going viral and it's ALREADY being mistranslated by news outlets. They never learn.
My friend abroad just texted asking if people still say that one word. They've been gone three months and they're already behind.
A brand just tried to use youth slang in an ad and the comments section is on fire. Corporate cringe is a universal language.
